clusters.title: Harsha faults lack of basic controls in $ 2.5 m Treasury breach

versions.title: Harsha faults lack of basic controls in $ 2.5 m Treasury breach
versions.deck: Opposition MP Dr. Harsha de Silva has criticised the reported $ 2.5 million Treasury fund breach, saying the incident appears to reflect basic negligence rather than a sophisticated cyber attack.

clusters.title: Mideast war: Can the crisis be turned into an opportunity?

versions.title: Mideast war: Can the crisis be turned into an opportunity?
versions.deck: Boosting morale by promising opportunities It has been fashionable for notable figures and political leaders to arouse positive sentiments among people deeply entrenched in crises, urging them not to lose faith but to use crises as opportunities to make a new start as a nation. clusters.title: Digital Economy Ministry says $ 2.5 m Treasury breach not system hack

versions.title: Digital Economy Ministry says $ 2.5 m Treasury breach not system hack
versions.deck: Appearing to highlight a significant distinction, Digital Economy Deputy Minister Eranga Weeraratne said the reported $ 2.5 million Treasury breach appears to be a sophisticated phishing and impersonation scam rather than a direct system hack. Speaking to media on Saturday, he said fraudsters had allegedly created a fake domain and email addresses resembling the international entity involved, using names similar to actual officials to mislead au

clusters.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet

versions.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s crisis is often described in familiar terms: excessive borrowing, weak fiscal discipline and unsustainable debt. This diagnosis is not wrong. But it is incomplete. At its core, the crisis reflects a deeper issue in how sovereign financial positions are assessed and managed. Fiscal analysis remains overwhelmingly focused on flows – deficits, debt servicing and financing needs – while the stock of assets underpinning the public sector

clusters.title: Sri Lanka welcomes over 100,000 tourists in first 23 days of April

versions.title: Sri Lanka welcomes over 100,000 tourists in first 23 days of April
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s tourism sector showed clear signs of cooling in April, even as the country edged close to the 100,000 mark in arrivals during the first three weeks of the month. According to the Sri Lanka Tourism Development Authority (SLTDA), the country welcomed 99,777 visitors between 1 and 22 April, marking a 26% decline year-on-year (YoY) compared to 133,979 arrivals in the same period last year. clusters.title: Energy supplies are crashing: What must Sri Lanka do?

versions.title: Energy supplies are crashing: What must Sri Lanka do?
versions.deck: According to Kpler, a global business intelligence company, seaborne crude and condensate exports were down 6.9 million bpd in March compared to the previous month, and are down another 1.8 million bpd through 20 April. This leads to the conclusion that loadings are likely to be down 8.8 million bpd from prewar levels. clusters.title: Choosing disaster over prevention and mitigation: Wilful blindness or short-sighted idiocy?

versions.title: Choosing disaster over prevention and mitigation: Wilful blindness or short-sighted idiocy?
versions.deck: Sri Lanka has been a serial victim of a brutal cocktail of natural and man-made macro disasters in recent times. These twin forces have not only claimed thousands of lives but have systematically crippled the country’s economic foundation, exposing a dangerous void in proactive national planning. This relentless series of disasters has ranged from devastating natural phenomena to man-made catastrophes. The 2004 Indian Ocean Tsunami, triggered by

clusters.title: Primary surplus almost doubles as Budget deficit nearly eliminated by end-Jan. 2026

versions.title: Primary surplus almost doubles as Budget deficit nearly eliminated by end-Jan. 2026
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s fiscal position strengthened markedly in January 2026, with a sharp expansion in the primary surplus and a near-elimination of the overall Budget deficit, according to data released by the Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL) last Friday. The Government recorded a primary surplus of Rs. 222.82 billion for the month, up 86.7% from Rs. 119.33 billion a year earlier, reflecting a continued improvement in revenue performance alongside restra

clusters.title: Abans Finance debenture issue closes today, initial Rs. 1 b offer oversubscribed

versions.title: Abans Finance debenture issue closes today, initial Rs. 1 b offer oversubscribed
versions.deck: Abans Finance PLC yesterday announced that its debenture issue has been oversubscribed, having received applications exceeding Rs. 1 billion for the initial tranche. The issue comprised an initial offering of 10 million listed, rated, senior, unsecured, redeemable debentures at a par value of Rs. 100 each, aiming to raise Rs. 1 billion, with an option to issue a further 5 million debentures to raise an additional Rs. 500 million in the event of

clusters.title: Endeavour of humanising technology: Enriching encounters

versions.title: Endeavour of humanising technology: Enriching encounters
versions.deck: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is all around us. Technology is making massive advances for both construction as well as destruction. In such a context, I enjoyed reading an “old but gold” book by Erich Fromm with an apt title “The Revolution of Hope.” The tag line shows us the essence of his endeavour “towards a humanised technology.” Today’s column reflects on its resounding insights relevant to a world ravaged by technology warfare with wide imp

clusters.title: Vehicle imports slow and fiscal boost tapers: JB Securities

versions.title: Vehicle imports slow and fiscal boost tapers: JB Securities
versions.deck: Vehicle imports slowed sharply in early 2026, with the sector’s contribution to growth and fiscal revenues beginning to taper even as March registrations rose on existing inventory, according to JB Securities. JB Securities Chief Executive Officer Murtaza Jafferjee said monthly vehicle imports have shown a clear slowdown this year, particularly in personal vehicles, with import values declining from a peak of $ 240.9 million in December 2025 to

clusters.title: Credit card base, balances see marginal growth YTD end-Feb.

versions.title: Credit card base, balances see marginal growth YTD end-Feb.
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s credit card base expanded in February, with the total number of active cards rising 1.3% compared to December 2025 to 2,193,380 from 2,166,186, according to latest data. The increase was driven by globally accepted cards, which grew 1.3% to 2,184,600 from 2,157,271. Locally accepted cards declined 1.5% to 8,780 from 8,915.

clusters.title: Beyond automation: Human skills powering finance in the AI era

versions.title: Beyond automation: Human skills powering finance in the AI era
versions.deck: Artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping economies, industries, and professions at an extraordinary pace. For finance professionals, this transformation is both a challenge and an opportunity. The question is no longer whether AI will affect finance, but how quickly professionals can adapt to remain relevant and valuable. Sri Lanka’s ambitions underscore the urgency. Our country has set a bold target for AI to contribute between $1.5 billion–1.

versions.deck: The story of Sea Consortium and its globally recognised brand, X-Press Feeders, is captured vividly in the commemorative volume Feeder Fever, co-authored by the company’s current Chairman, Tim Hartnoll. clusters.title: Underdog Asanga wins NOC SL Presidency beating hyped-up Jaswar, Shirantha

versions.title: Underdog Asanga wins NOC SL Presidency beating hyped-up Jaswar, Shirantha
versions.deck: Multiple sports personality Asanga Seneviratne on Saturday clinched the Presidency of the National Olympic Committee of Sri Lanka (NOC SL), beating the much hyped-up Shirantha Peiris and Jaswar Umar. As the Weekend FT on Saturday described him as an underdog (https://www.ft.lk/sports/Battle-heats-up-between-Jaswar-and-Shirantha-for-NOCSL-Presidency/23-791117), Asanga secured 16 votes in a convincing victory. clusters.title: Govt. flags negligence and vulnerability in Treasury payments after $ 2.5 m theft

versions.title: Govt. flags negligence and vulnerability in Treasury payments after $ 2.5 m theft
versions.deck: Industry and Entrepreneurship Development Deputy Minister Chathuranga Abeysinghe has pointed to “negligence and vulnerability in Treasury payments” following the $ 2.5 million cyber breach linked to a Government debt transaction, framing the incident as part of broader systemic weaknesses in public sector processes.

clusters.title: Sri Lanka display dominance over Bangladesh

versions.title: Sri Lanka display dominance over Bangladesh
versions.deck: Sri Lanka Women displayed their dominance over Bangladesh Women in the ODI series when they won their second bilateral series against them 2-1 at Rajshahi. The two countries had met on an earlier occasion in a bilateral series three years ago in Sri Lanka when the home team emerged victorious 1-0. versions.deck: The Ceylon Electricity Board (CEB) remained under financial strain in 2025 despite cost-reflective tariff adjustments, the Central Bank said in its Annual Economic Review.

clusters.title: Trumpflation and recession

versions.title: Trumpflation and recession
versions.deck: Trumpflation has two components, inflation and stagflation, which ultimately lead to recession. It is a negative shock to the world economy.

versions.title: Emerging Asia could face increased food-cost pressures: Fitch
versions.deck: Emerging markets in Asia would see rising cost pressure across agribusiness and food supply chains if a prolonged US-Iran war pushes fertiliser supply disruption deeper into the planting season, Fitch Ratings says.
